pr.title: Misc gm_19EF, mn_2295, and gmtitle matches
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Matched a batch of title-screen, menu, and gm_19EF functions, with gmtitle made functional enough in nonmatching builds to move src/melee/gm/gmtitle.c from NonMatching to Equivalent. The bot reported 14 new matches (+3396 bytes, +0.09% matched code), including gmTitle_801A12C4, gmTitle_801A1AC0, gmTitle_801A1C18_OnFrame, gm_801A0C6C_OnEnter, fn_801A0B60, and several mn_2295 functions. The PR also tightened prototypes, added archive/baselib helpers, fixed symbol splitting for gmtitle data/strings, and made small matching-support cleanups in Yoshi init, lbarq, aobj/jobj/robj headers.
what_changed: Changed configure.py classification for melee/gm/gmtitle.c from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Equivalent, ...); the PR body explicitly says gmtitle is fully functional in a nonmatching build.

Implemented large parts of src/melee/gm/gmtitle.c: title model/background setup, camera/light/fog setup, archive symbol loading via gmTitle_801A1AC0, title OnFrame input handling via gmTitle_801A1C18_OnFrame, debug build timestamp conversion/display helper gmTitle_801A1D38, and OnEnter setup for audio, MTHP/title assets, and scene objects.
Added or improved gmtitle animation helpers using HSD_JObjReqAnimAll/HSD_JObjAnimAll and mn_8022ED6C/mn_8022F298, including a static inline setup helper for the title background transition.

Implemented gm_19EF scene setup/teardown functions for the apparent 'Go!'/'Coming Soon' animation flow: fn_801A0B60, gm_801A0C6C_OnEnter, and gm_801A0E0C_OnLeave. These load IfComSn.dat, create CObj/LObj/JObj GObjs, install a proc, test animation end frames, then free the archive and reset audio on leave.

Implemented several mn_2295 menu functions, including mn_80229894, mn_80229938, mn_80229A04, mn_80229A7C, mn_80229B2C, mn_80229DC0, and mn_8022DD38_OnFrame, and improved mn_8022ED6C from void to float-returning animation-frame helper.
Updated mn_2295.h and gmtitle.h away from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS toward concrete HSD_GObj*, HSD_Archive*, bool, int, void, u16, and Vec3-based prototypes.

Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt to correct/split gmtitle rodata/data symbols, including converting gmTitle_803B7D50/gmTitle_803B7D5C to local string labels and splitting gmTitle_803DA4FC plus many following string labels from a formerly oversized data region.
Added lbArchive_80016F80 prototype to lbarchive.h and tightened lbArq_80014ABC to return s32 with UNK_T arg in lbarq.c/h.
Added HSD_AObjGetCurrFrame inline helper and converted HSD_AObjGetEndFrame to use HSD_ASSERT. This supported gm_19EF animation-end checks through aobj->curr_frame/end_frame.
Removed unnecessary baselib/aobj.h includes from jobj.h and robj.h, and made resolveCnsOrientation static in robj.c.
Adjusted ftYs_Init.c matching details by moving the __FILE__ override after includes and shrinking a stack padding array from 20 to 12 bytes.
smart_moves: Used Equivalent instead of Matching for gmtitle, preserving build progress while acknowledging the object is functionally correct but not fully byte-matching.
Let the decomp-dev bot quantify impact: 14 new matches and four improved unmatched functions, making the PR’s matching value easy to audit.
Split symbol-table data/string regions around gmtitle rather than leaving a broad float/data blob; this made newly introduced globals and strings line up with the code being decompiled.
Converted only exported functions to header prototypes and kept helpers static in C where appropriate, reducing header noise and accidental external linkage.
Introduced small baselib accessors such as HSD_AObjGetCurrFrame instead of open-coding field reads at call sites; this mirrors existing HSD_AObjGetEndFrame style and improves reuse.
Used asset-symbol loading names directly in gmTitle_801A1AC0, documenting the archive contract for GmTtAll.dat/GmTtAll.usd and the title model/camera/light/fog resources.
Reused menu animation helper mn_8022ED6C from gmtitle code instead of duplicating frame wrap/clamp logic.
Included precise sysdolphin/melee headers in changed C files, which allowed prototype improvements and reduced reliance on placeholder/UNK patterns.
decomp_lessons: When an object is operationally correct in a nonmatching build but not fully matched, marking it Equivalent can be a valid intermediate milestone if evidenced by the PR and build report.
For scene setup functions, matching often depends on modeling the GObj creation order, GX link priorities, JObj animation setup, proc registration, and archive symbol loading sequence exactly.
Animation helpers may need to return the current frame even if earlier stubs used void; downstream callers can expose the true signature.
Symbol-table granularity matters: incorrect large data ranges can hide adjacent strings/floats and block correct global declarations in source.
Header cleanup is part of matching work: replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with concrete types often unlocks better call expressions and reduces casts or placeholder usage.
For HSD/baselib decomp, local inline accessors and HSD_ASSERT line numbers can be important for matching and should mirror library style.
Small stack and __FILE__ placement changes, as in ftYs_Init.c, can be legitimate matching tactics even outside the main PR target.
Do not export file-local helpers: converting resolveCnsOrientation to static and omitting static gmtitle helpers from headers reflects a cleaner linkage model.
naming_conventions: Game-mode functions retain address-derived names with subsystem prefixes, e.g. gmTitle_801A12C4, gm_801A0C6C_OnEnter, mn_80229B2C.
Lifecycle functions use _OnEnter, _OnLeave, and _OnFrame suffixes when evidence shows scene/frame callbacks.
File-local unknown globals keep lbl_ address names, e.g. lbl_804D66F0/lbl_804D66F4/lbl_804D66F8 in gm_19EF.c.
Confirmed gmtitle globals use gmTitle_ address names for meaningful data vectors/descriptors, e.g. gmTitle_803DA4F0, gmTitle_803DA4FC, gmTitle_803DA508, gmTitle_80479B28.
Local string/data labels in symbols.txt use lbl_ addresses rather than incorrectly global gmTitle_ names when no semantic name is established.
Static helper names may remain fn_ address names where purpose is unclear, e.g. fn_801A1498 and fn_80229BF4.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK adjustments in gmTitle_801A1E20_OnEnter, gmTitle_801A19AC, gm_801A0C6C_OnEnter, and ftYs_Init_8012B6E8 to satisfy stack layout/matching constraints.
Moved ftYs_Init.c __FILE__ macro after includes to retain ftyoshi.c assert/debug identity without disturbing earlier include-generated code layout.
Casted HSD_AObj frame values to int in gm_19EF before comparing current and end frame, matching the apparent original logic.
Used boolean normalization patterns like explicit true/false assignment around gm_801A4310/gm_801A42C4 checks in gmtitle animation setup.
Used local temps to avoid repeated mn_8022F298 calls in mn_8022ED6C, improving match while preserving animation wrap/clamp behavior.
Kept asset loading varargs order in lbArchive_LoadSymbols aligned with archive symbol names; this is critical for title-screen scene setup.
Converted mn_80229938 from s32 returns to bool while preserving branch behavior; this made call sites and counting logic clearer.
Added HSD_AObjGetCurrFrame/HSD_AObjGetEndFrame accessors with HSD_ASSERT line constants, a common baselib matching concern.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
The decomp-dev bot reported GALE01 matched code increasing to 35.25% (+0.09%, +3396 bytes).

Bot-listed new matches: gmTitle_801A12C4, gm_801A0C6C_OnEnter, fn_801A1498, gmTitle_801A1AC0, gmTitle_801A1C18_OnFrame, fn_801A0B60, gmTitle_801A1D38, mn_80229B2C, mn_80229A7C, mn_80229894, mn_80229A04, mn_8022DD38_OnFrame, gm_801A0E0C_OnLeave, and mn_80229938.
Bot-listed improved unmatched functions: gmTitle_801A1E20_OnEnter to 96.89%, gmTitle_801A165C to 87.27%, mn_80229DC0 to 91.67%, and mn_8022ED6C to 95.71%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that move src/melee/gm/gmtitle.c from Equivalent to Matching or finish gmTitle_801A1E20_OnEnter/gmTitle_801A165C.
Search for subsequent mn_2295 matches involving mn_80229DC0, mn_80229F60, mn_8022DB10, and mn_8022EE84.
Check whether lbArchive_80016F80 was implemented or just prototyped in later library cleanup PRs.
Look for additional symbol-table corrections around gmTitle_803DA4F0/gmTitle_803DA4FC/gmTitle_803DA508 and title-screen strings.
Search future baselib PRs for HSD_AObjGetCurrFrame, HSD_AObjGetEndFrame, and include dependency cleanup in jobj.h/robj.h.
